note: Authoritative local data from L1 chains.db (DeFiLlama + CoinGecko + growthepie/Dune). Raw signals, no scores. null = NO DATA (do not guess). FRESHNESS: price & market caps are live (today); TVL/stablecoin LEVELS are the latest available (carried forward if a chain's today-point isn't in yet); daily-FLOW metrics (DEX volume, fees, active_addresses, tx_count) are the LAST COMPLETE UTC DAY (today is partial and excluded) — full-day figures, use them as-is. SHARES are each chain's % of the WHOLE DeFi market (all ~500 chains; denominators in market.total_*), so tracked chains sum to <100% and deltas are immune to which chains we track. TVL/volume are USD-denominated, so share moves in a big market swing partly reflect token price, not pure flows. Each chain ALSO carries its OWN raw TVL growth — tvl_change_1d/7d/30d_pct (chain's level vs itself N days ago); use THESE for 'is this chain's TVL growing', NOT the share deltas (share = slice of market, a different question). If tvl_suspect_1d is true the 1d move is a likely upstream data glitch (a >25% one-day swing) — discount the 1d figure and lean on the 7d/30d trend instead.

emerging_candidates.method: longshot-style L1/L2 momentum + DefiLlama TVL growth, radar excluded
emerging_candidates.how_to_use: RAW watchlist of non-radar chains rising on the signals below — NOT buy calls. A candidate becomes a LEAD only on confluence (also trending / in a heating narrative / has a catalyst / fresh dev-gov) or a confirmed on-chain TVL surge.

signal_glossary.volume_heat: Volume is large vs market cap (turnover ≥15%) — unusual interest building.
signal_glossary.vol_leads_price: Volume is hot but 7d price hasn't run yet — interest building BEFORE the move (the early sweet spot).
signal_glossary.momentum: 7d gain keeping pace with / exceeding 30d — the move is accelerating, not fading.
signal_glossary.low_float: Only a small share of supply circulates — CAUTION: unlock overhang can cap upside.
signal_glossary.tvl_surge: On-chain TVL jumped ≥25% in 7d from a small base — real capital arriving, not just token price.

narrative.method: CoinGecko category momentum (24h mcap change + constituent 7d breadth/median); thematic narratives only (chain-ecosystem/stablecoin categories excluded)

events.subject: Plasma (XPL) US-purchaser unlock cliff
events.note: 2.5B XPL = 25% of total supply; zero-fee chain has no offsetting capture

catalysts.note: Curated dated catalysts (edit catalysts.json). DeFiLlama unlocks API is Pro-gated; the routine news-scan supplements. Fold these into the Open Items ledger.

attention.source: CoinGecko /search/trending (search/view velocity = retail attention proxy; LunarCrush free tier is locked/402)

pre_winner_lookalikes.method: validated winner-DNA coil screen (deep-below-high + oversold + at-30d-low [all validated, hold OOS] + below-ATH/high-vol/mid-float/small-cap); primed-but-not-yet-moved. NECESSARY-not-sufficient setup — needs an event trigger.
pre_winner_lookalikes.how_to_use: Beaten-down coils matching the data-validated winner DNA. SETUPS, not buys: most never move. Edge comes ONLY from confluence — a lookalike that is also trending / in a heating narrative / has a near catalyst / has fresh dev-gov.

signal_glossary.deep_below_60d_high: Trading <50% of its 60-day high — deeply beaten down. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample).
signal_glossary.oversold_30d: Down >25% over 30 days — oversold. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample).
signal_glossary.at_30d_low: Trading within ~3% of its 30-day low RIGHT NOW — the capitulation launch zone. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample); a bounce off the low actually LOWERS winner odds, so this is a timing edge.
signal_glossary.oversold_90d: Down >40% over 90 days — sustained downtrend (supporting context).
signal_glossary.deep_below_ath: Trading <15% of all-time high — far from prior peak, room to recover.
signal_glossary.high_vol: Elevated 30d volatility — capable of large moves.
signal_glossary.mid_float: Meaningful but not-fully-diluted float — the winner sweet spot.
signal_glossary.small_cap: Sub-$100M market cap — small enough to multiply.

dev_governance.note: Pre-announcement layer (early, low-crowd): GitHub releases/commit-activity + Snapshot governance for tracked protocols. A fresh_release or a pending 'launch/fund X' proposal precedes the marketing post. Each item carries release_notes / body (truncated) + a keyword dimension_hint (raw, NOT a verdict) — the FOLLOW-UP is to read these and judge what the change actually does and whether it improves the protocol at a fundamental level (consensus/scaling/security/economics/new capability) vs routine housekeeping. No composite score.

dev.release_notes:
## What's Changed
* Backport `release/v6.6`: Add GoReleaser release pipeline for static seid binaries (#3425) by @monty-sei in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3694
* Backport `release/v6.6`: Fix rpc hash race condition by @seidroid[bot] in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3697
* Backport `release/v6.6`: Accept legacy cosmos_only SC write mode by @seidroid[bot] in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3717
* Backport `release/v6.6`: Update v6.6 changelog in prep to cut rc2  by @seidroid[bot] in https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/pull/3731


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/sei-protocol/sei-chain/compare/v6.6.0-rc1...v6.6.0-rc2

```
CODE_COLOR: CODE_YELLOW_MAINNET
RELEASE_VERSION: 2.13.0
PROTOCOL_UPGRADE: TRUE
DATABASE_UPGRADE: TRUE
SECURITY_UPGRADE: FALSE
```

# [NOTE] Check your `state_sync.sync` setting in config.json
2.13 no longer accepts the old centralized (external-storage) state sync. If your `config.json` has `state_sync.sync` set to `ExternalStorage`, like the following:
```
{
  "state_sync": {
    "sync": {
      "ExternalStorage": {
        "location": { "GCS": { "bucket": "..." } },
        "num_concurrent_requests": 4,
        "external_storage_fallback_threshold": 5
      }
    }
  }
}
```
`neard` will exit on startup with:

`external-storage (centralized) state sync ('state_sync.sync.ExternalStorage') is no longer supported...`

You do not need to delete the whole `state_sync` block. Only the `sync` field matters:

* Remove the `state_sync.sync` block, **or** set `"state_sync": { "sync": "Peers" }`.
* `state_sync.dump`, `state_sync.concurrency`, and `state_sync.parts_compression_lvl` can stay and still work. (`dump` will log a deprecation warning but does not block startup.)

If you never configured `state_sync.sync` (the default), no action is needed. Peer-to-peer state sync is the default and only supported path.

# Protocol Changes
* Opt-in strict nonce mode for transactions. A transaction may opt in to require its nonce be exactly the previous nonce for that access key plus one; such transactions are rejected when the nonce repeats or skips a …[truncated]

## Protocol
#### Sui Protocol Version in this release: `129`

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27096: Introduces a new protocol version that adds explicit bounds for binary pools that were otherwise implicitly constrained before. No user-visible change.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/26914: adds `insert_before` and `insert_after` to `sui::linked_table`

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27067: use `defining_ids` in `sui::coin`

## Nodes (Validators and Full nodes)

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27063: the state-sync archive fallback now supports `gs://` (Google Cloud Storage) ingestion URLs. A new `remote-store-headers` option under `state-archive-read-config` attaches default HTTP headers to archive requests, which enables requester-pays GCS buckets via `x-goog-user-project`. Previously only `file` / `s3` / `http` archive stores were supported.

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27026: New optional `RUST_LOG_TAILS` env var additionally writes individual tracing targets to dedicated files (format inferred from the file extension — `.jsonl`/`.ndjson` for JSON, otherwise text), leaving normal log output unchanged. Backward-compatible — unset by default, so log output is unchanged and no action is required.

## gRPC

https://github.com/MystenLabs/sui/pull/27035: Opaque pagination cursors returned by the scanning apis `list_transactions`, `list_events`, and `list_checkpoints` have a new internal encoding. Cursors remain opaque and clients should …[truncated]

This is a testnet release.

https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/blob/v4.2/CHANGELOG.md

# What's Changed

* transaction-status: fix parsed account labels for confidential transfer `Deposit` and `Withdraw` by @gitteri in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13149
* blockstore: Remove bincode support in Rewards column by @steviez in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13196
* rpc-client: Add method to get latest blockhash with response context (notably `context.slot`) by @GabrielePicco in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/12826
* XDP by default by @alexpyattaev in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/12119
* xdp: use af-xdp for gossip egress traffic by @KirillLykov in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13141
* cleanup(accounts-db): rip out StorageAccess::Mmap by @kskalski in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/12443
* Replaces --accounts-db-cache-limit-mb with --accounts-db-write-cache-limit by @brooksprumo in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13251
* Promote PoH pinned CPU core flag by @gregcusack in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/13191
* turbine: tighter filter of shred admission by @alexpyattaev in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/12654
* Update cli to support alpenglow votes and epoch credits by @akhi3030 in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/12872
* remote-wallet: support keystone by @ww3512687 in https://github.com/anza-xyz/agave/pull/11944

**Full Changelog**: …[truncated]

This release is available as a Docker Image on Docker Hub at `offchainlabs/nitro-node:v3.11.2-3599aca`
This Docker image specifies default flags in its entrypoint which should be replicated if you're overriding the entrypoint: `/usr/local/bin/nitro --validation.wasm.allowed-wasm-module-roots /home/user/nitro-legacy/machines,/home/user/target/machines\`

If you're running a validator without a split validation server (this will be true of most validators), you should instead use the image offchainlabs/nitro-node:v3.11.2-3599aca-validator which has the extra script `/usr/local/bin/split-val-entry.sh` as the entrypoint.

## [v3.11.2](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/compare/v3.11.1...v3.11.2) - 2026-06-30

### Configuration

- Add `--node.dangerous.always-fallback-to-parent-chain-da` to support retiring chains off AnyTrust. [[commit]](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/commit/d71b69788edf8fd41385dcfadd9c8ee3c5ab1481)
- Add `--execution.transaction-filtering.address-filter.s3.preallocate-memory` (default true) to preallocate and commit the hash-list memory at startup, engaging only when `s3.max-file-size-mb` is set. [[commit]](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/commit/1ca3775819688f9211f96bb8a33ad5bcc10aae9c)

### Added

- Added consensus-v61 to dockerfile, remove rc and older consensus. [[commit]](https://github.com/OffchainLabs/nitro/commit/e53631372daa8627f8ef086324bb5e3457ee1b46)

### Changed

- Default `--file-logging.max-backups` increased from …[truncated]

Valencia hardfork release for the Polygon PoS Bor execution client.

## Activation

| Network | Block |
| --- | --- |
| Amoy | 40,776,000 |
| Mainnet | 89,531,000 |

## What's Changed
* Release v2.9.0 by @kamuikatsurgi in https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/pull/2295


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/0xPolygon/bor/compare/v2.8.3...v2.9.0

## What's Changed

> [!WARNING]
> **All node operators must upgrade to op-node/v1.19.1 or later**. See the [op-node/v1.19.1 release notes](https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/releases/tag/op-node%2Fv1.19.1) for more information about Karst activation and its incompatibility with older versions of op-reth. All of the callouts on that release page apply.

This release fixes an issue encountered when using op-node with an EL that prunes historical bodies (e.g., when using op-reth with `--minimal` or `--prune.bodies.distance`).

## What's Changed in op-node/v1.19.2
* fix(op-node): tolerate EL-expired genesis history during L2 config validation by @einar-oplabs in #21587 

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/compare/op-node/v1.19.1...op-node/v1.19.2

🚢 Docker Image: https://us-docker.pkg.dev/oplabs-tools-artifacts/images/op-node:v1.19.2

![image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/berachain/beacon-kit/refs/heads/main/.github/assets/banner.png)

## Summary

This is the **mainnet-ready Fusaka (Fulu) release** for BeaconKit. It is the first stable cut of the `v1.4.x` line and bundles the full Fulu fork along with a round of CometBFT security fixes and node API hardening.

- **Mainnet Fusaka (Fulu) hard fork.** Sets the mainnet Fulu fork time and ships everything the fork requires: the [BRIP-0008](https://github.com/berachain/BRIPs) effective-balance hysteresis update, EIP-6110 style in-protocol deposit processing, the new Fulu EVM inflation values, and corrected EL genesis configs. **All mainnet operators must upgrade to this version before the fork activates.**
- **CometBFT security fixes.** Bumps CometBFT to a version that includes additional consensus-layer fixes, on top of the earlier `maxPeerHeight` poisoning fix.
- **Node API hardening.** Bounds previously unbounded user input on the filter and proof APIs (validator index / filter inputs), closing a resource-exhaustion vector.
- **Engine & resilience fixes.** The engine client now classifies EL HTTP 4xx responses as fatal via a typed `HTTPStatusError`, and retries on EL connection errors to survive execution-client restarts.
- **Performance.** Adds a validator set cache to avoid a full state scan in `GetValidators`.

This version is **required for the mainnet Fusaka hard fork** occurring at **July 8th 16:00:00 UTC** (unix `1783526400`). The …[truncated]

## What Changed

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Base Mainnet**: Operators must upgrade to this release by June 25th at 18:00:00 UTC for the Beryl hardfork. See [here](https://docs.base.org/base-chain/specs/upgrades/beryl/overview) for more information.
> **Base Sepolia**: Recommended upgrade to improve Flashblocks stability

### base-reth-node
- Beryl Support for Mainnet
- Fix Flashblock processing where transactions relying on the BLOCKHASH could have different execution outcomes during Flashblocks and canonical processing.

### base-consensus
- Beryl Support for Mainnet

governance.title: OAT Elections
governance.body:
The election phase for the Oversight and Transparency Committee (OAT) will begin today, Thursday 9th of July, and will conclude a week later on Thursday, 16th of July. For the vote, a shutterized, weighted voting system will be utilized.

All candidates that applied have passed the preliminary eligibility check and will therefore participate in the elections. In the event that an elected candidate fails to pass the full compliance process after their election, the seat will be offered to the next eligible candidate by votes received.

The applicants are (in order of their application):

- [Manoj Kumar Desai](https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/oversight-and-transparency-committee-oat-june-2026-elections-application-thread/30873/2)
- [Max Lomu](https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/oversight-and-transparency-committee-oat-june-2026-elections-application-thread/30873/3)
- [Federico Holzwarth](https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/oversight-and-transparency-committee-oat-june-2026-elections-application-thread/30873/4)
- [Pedro Breuer](https://forum.arbitrum.foundation/t/oversight-and-transparency-committee-oat-june-2026-elections-application-thread/30873/5)
- [Patrick …[truncated]

governance.body:
Quorum: 6.68m CVX 



Add a gauge for the following pool: sUSG/reUSD





Action 1
----------
To: Curve Gauge Controller
Function: AddGauge
Gauge: 0xc58fb163359b8e53C628868BA6C9335D1A6fD16B (https://etherscan.io/address/0xc58fb163359b8e53C628868BA6C9335D1A6fD16B)

governance.title: [6.47] [Social] Election of the New ENS DAO Security Council
governance.body:
| Status | Active |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Type** | Social |
| **Discussion Thread** | [Discuss](https://discuss.ens.domains/t/draft-social-proposal-for-a-new-security-council/22219) |

## Abstract

The two-year mandate of the current Security Council expires soon. The framework was established under [EP 5.7](https://docs.ens.domains/dao/proposals/5.7/), the members were confirmed under [EP 5.10](https://docs.ens.domains/dao/proposals/5.10/), and the contract was deployed under [EP 5.13](https://docs.ens.domains/dao/proposals/5.13/).

This proposal asks the DAO to elect the 8 members of a successor Security Council. The successor Council will operate under a tighter public mandate, a binding Appointment Agreement with the ENS Foundation, a removal mechanism for members who act outside the mandate, and a 5/8 threshold for action. The full framework, rationale, and nomination process were set out in the [draft proposal](https://discuss.ens.domains/t/draft-social-proposal-for-a-new-security-council/22219). The Security Council Charter, the Appointment Agreement, and the Public Pledge are available …[truncated]

governance.title: [Temp Check] Activate v4 Protocol Fees
governance.body:
## Summary

This proposal continues the protocol fee rollout approved in UNIfication, following proposals [#93](https://vote.uniswapfoundation.org/proposals/93), [#94](https://vote.uniswapfoundation.org/proposals/94), [#95](https://vote.uniswapfoundation.org/proposals/95), and [#96](https://vote.uniswapfoundation.org/proposals/96). It uses the expedited governance process where fee parameter update proposals go directly to a five-day Snapshot followed by an onchain vote.

Protocol fees are now live across all v2 and v3 pools on 11 chains - Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base, Celo, OP Mainnet, Soneium, X Layer, Worldchain, Zora, BNB Chain, and Polygon. Last month, the protocol set a record burning [186,000 UNI in one day](https://x.com/UNIBurnBot/status/2063252718043459633).

Below we introduce a system for v4 protocol fees and propose to activate it on a subset of v4 pools on these same chains.

---

## Implementation Details

v4's hook architecture requires a different approach to fee activation than v2 or v3. v2 pools have a single LP fee tier and are charged a static fee. v3 has several LP fee tiers, each charged a static fee. Hooks mean v4 has potentially infinite distinct LP fee tiers, …[truncated]
